title: Dynamic Content Generation for Exercises, Quizzes, and Exams from JSON Data
body:
As a system developer, I want all exercises, pop-quizzes, and exams to pull their vocabulary and sentence structures directly from our local JSON files, so that content changes can be managed centrally through data updates without rewriting application logic.

Acceptance Criteria
1. Data-Driven Content Generation

All interactive assessment elements—including exercises, pop-quizzes, and milestone exams—must be dynamically generated using only the words and sentences present in the application's .json data files.

Hardcoded text strings or manual content blocks within the application component views must be removed.

2. Content Matching & Validation

The generation engine must strictly pair definitions, vocabulary words, translation tasks, and sentence puzzles to the exact content blocks mapped out in the JSON file for that specific lesson or module.

Build a validation fallback to ensure that if a JSON block is missing expected variables (like a missing translation string), the system fails gracefully rather than rendering empty text spaces.

3. Test and Quiz Logic Integrity

Pop-quizzes and exams must dynamically pull correct answers and corresponding distractor options (wrong answers) directly from the context of the JSON dataset.

Randomized matching logic must ensure that test variants pull from the assigned pool of JSON content mapped to the user's current progress milestone.

title: Fix Streak Shield (Streak Pajzs) Rendering, Purchase Logic, and Validation
body:
As a learner on the platform,
I want my Streak Shields (Streak Pajzsok) to accurately reflect how many shields I own (up to a maximum of 3),
So that I can confidently know my daily streak is protected if I miss a day of practicing English.

Acceptance Criteria (AC)
1. Visual Rendering Fix (The 3rd Shield Bug)
•	Given a user has purchased or earned 3 Streak Shields,
•	When they look at the header section in the right sidebar,
•	Then all 3 shield icons must render as fully active/filled. (Currently, the third icon never shows as full, even if the backend count is 3).
•	Then if a user has 0, 1, or 2 shields, the remaining slots up to the max capacity of 3 must render as empty/inactive placeholders.
2. Purchase & Inventory Logic
•	Given a user is in the Reward Shop (JUTALOM BOLT),
•	When they click "Buy" (Vásárlás) on the Streak Shield item,
•	Then the system must check if current_shields < 3.
•	If true: Deduct the cost, increment the shield count in the database (Supabase/MariaDB), and update the UI.
•	If false (already has 3): The "Vásárlás" button should be disabled, or a message should state that shield inventory is full.
3. Streak Protection Logic (The "Missed Day" Rules)
•	When a user opens the app for the first time on a new calendar day:
•	Scenario A (Active): If they completed a module yesterday, their active streak day count increments or holds.
•	Scenario B (Protected): If they missed yesterday but have shields > 0, the system must consume exactly 1 shield (shields - 1), maintain the active streak count, and notify the user that a shield saved their streak.
•	Scenario C (Broken): If they missed yesterday and have shields == 0, the active streak resets to 0.

Hardcoded Test Scenario (Local Development Setup)
To help your junior developer test edge cases safely without waiting 48 real-world hours, have them implement a temporary mock state toggle in the code.
•	The Setup: Create a hardcoded mock state that simulates the following data timeline when the component mounts:
•	Day 1 to 10: User builds a 10-day streak (saved in DB).
•	Day 11: User has 3 Streak Shields in inventory.
•	Day 12 & 13: User completely logs off and misses both days.
•	The Expected Outcome to Verify: * When the app is opened on Day 14, the user should see their 10-day streak is still intact.
•	The right sidebar must now display exactly 1 active shield left (2 shields were consumed to protect the 2 missed days).
🛠️ Technical Context & Notes (For Junior Dev)
•	Check the Loop/Array Index: The bug where the "third shield is never full" is highly likely a classic off-by-one error in the UI rendering loop. Look at the component drawing the shields (e.g., a .map() or a for loop checking index < streakShields). If it's using a strict less-than operator against a 0-indexed layout, the 3rd item might be failing the condition.
•	State Synchronization: Ensure that when a shield is bought in the shop column, the state in the header column updates instantly without requiring a full page refresh.

Why This Matters to a Junior (Teaching Moment)
"Gamification features like streaks and shields drive user retention, but they rely entirely on trust. If a user loses a 50-day streak because our code didn't accurately register their third shield, they will feel cheated and might quit the app. This is why writing hardcoded time-travel test scenarios is an industry standard: we can't wait days in real life to see if our time and date logic actually works!"
